Security Tip: 2. Vector Stores Are a First-Class Attack Surface (DSGAI13)

Why You're Probably Ignoring It Your team probably treats vector databases as "just another data store" — if they think about them at all. But most production vector store deployments lack authentication entirely. Collections are accessible from internal networks without encryption. And the security

- Audit vector DB authentication. If your Qdrant, Pinecone, or Weaviate instance h
- Enable TLS between your application and vector store.
- Add vector stores to your asset inventory and patch management cycle.

posts.section: 3. Non-Human Identity Sprawl from AI Agents (DSGAI02)

instagram.caption:
Security tip: 3. Non-Human Identity Sprawl from AI Agents (DSGAI02)

Why You're Probably Ignoring It Every AI agent that calls an API needs credentials. When Agent A spawns Agent B to complete a subtask, Agent B needs its own API keys, service accounts, or OAuth tokens...

- Inventory all NHIs (Non-Human Identities) created by or for AI agents.
- Enforce short-lived tokens over long-lived API keys. If an agent credential does
- Add AI agent credentials to your PAM lifecycle. If they're not in your credentia

#cybersecurity #infosec #securityplus #comptia #fixthevuln #itsecurity #cybersecuritytips #learninfosec #3.nonhumanidentitysp

facebook.post:
Security Tip: 3. Non-Human Identity Sprawl from AI Agents (DSGAI02)

Why You're Probably Ignoring It Every AI agent that calls an API needs credentials. When Agent A spawns Agent B to complete a subtask, Agent B needs its own API keys, service accounts, or OAuth tokens. These multiply rapidly — often with overprivileged access and no expiration. Your PAM system doesn

- Inventory all NHIs (Non-Human Identities) created by or for AI agents.
- Enforce short-lived tokens over long-lived API keys. If an agent credential does
- Add AI agent credentials to your PAM lifecycle. If they're not in your credentia

Read the full article: https://fixthevuln.com/blog/owasp-genai-data-security-guide-2026.html

Security tip: 4. Derived Artifacts Carry Reconstructable Data (DSGAI18)

Why You're Probably Ignoring It Your DLP policies cover databases, file shares, and email. But do they cover embeddings and fine-tuned model weights ? Membership inference attacks can determine whethe...

- Reclassify embeddings and fine-tuned models as sensitive data — because they con
- Include model artifacts in data retention policies. If you delete a customer's r
- Run a membership inference audit on your fine-tuned models before deploying them

Security Tip: 4. Derived Artifacts Carry Reconstructable Data (DSGAI18)

Why You're Probably Ignoring It Your DLP policies cover databases, file shares, and email. But do they cover embeddings and fine-tuned model weights ? Membership inference attacks can determine whether a specific individual's data was used in training. Model inversion can reconstruct training exampl

- Reclassify embeddings and fine-tuned models as sensitive data — because they con
- Include model artifacts in data retention policies. If you delete a customer's r
- Run a membership inference audit on your fine-tuned models before deploying them
